mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces market participants to price in higher future interest rates, which compresses equity valuation multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the inflation acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and increase in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60d SPX returns in > 55% of cases over a 5-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent quarter-over-quarter signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2 percent coincides with negative SPX 20d returns in > 60 percent of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strong residential investment and construction sector activity, which correlates positively with broader economic momentum and equity market performance.
failure_condition: HOUST increases precede negative 5d SPX returns in > 50 percent of cases over a 5-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 3 percent quarter-over-quarter signals strong aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 3 percent is followed by negative 60d SPX returns in > 55 percent of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate falling below 5 percent signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressures and consumer spending power, driving positive equity returns in expansionary phases.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 5 percent coincides with negative 20d SPX returns in > 60 percent of observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: High household savings combined with rising industrial production indicates pent-up demand meeting recovering supply capacity, generating positive equity returns as corporate earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60d SPX returns in > 50 percent of triggered instances over a 4-year evaluation period.
